things to do in south africa
videos.transcript: Johannesburg affectionately called joburg Josie and egoli the City of Gold is the financial and Industrial Metropolis of South Africa built on a rich history of gold mining the city is rapidly evolving from an edgy Safari stopover to a vibrant hub for arts and culture cutting-edge contemporary Galleries and the new mabening Precinct with its funky restaurants cafes and art studios now rank among the city's top tourist attractions along with the poignant apartheid Museum and Constitution Hill glaring disparities The Shanty towns of Soweto birthplace of the nation struggle for democracy still contrast sharply with the walled Villas of wealthy enclaves and the city's gleaming skyscrapers however rapid changes are coaxing tourists to linga in this video we're going to be taking a look at the top 10 rated attractions and things to do in Johannesburg and just wait till you see what's at number one that we're going to be showing in this video something you would never even have thought of so make sure you want watch till the end before we begin you can help support our Channel by becoming a member of this channel press the join button below this will help us to bring you more awesome travel videos so now if you're ready let's cut to the chase at 10 Walter sisulu National botanical gardens if you want to escape the hustle and bustle of the city and immerse yourself in nature the Walter sisulu National botanical gardens offer a Serene slice of Wilderness on the edge of the city managed by the South African National biodiversity Institute the garden is one of the nine National botanical gardens in South Africa and this particular location offers plenty of space and beautiful views of Red Rock Cliffs The Gardener centerpiece is the impressive wood porcher waterfall sprawling Fields surround the falls with plenty of prime picnic nooks and a river and hiking trails weave through the well-tended gardens highlights include the cycad garden Fern Trail succulent Rockery Garden the birds and butterfly garden and the geological display garden birders and photographers will appreciate the bird hide and kids can burn off steam in the Children's Garden keep an eye out for the Park's abundant birds and insects including black Eagles that nest in the park foreign day trip to Pretoria about 55 kilometers from Johannesburg Pretoria is the administrative capital of South Africa and is worth visiting for its impressive lineup of historical buildings monuments and museums Ablaze with the purple Hues of jacarandas in the spring Pretoria is also a city of beautiful parks and Gardens highlights of a visit here include the Pretoria National botanic garden the ruatrekker monument Freedom Park the large zoo and a clutch of peaceful nature reserves within the city limits with diverse wildlife and abundance of birds next up at eight pilansburg National Park day trip an easily accessible two and a half hour drive from Johannesburg polansburg National Park is one of the most popular game reserves in South Africa you have a chance to see Africa's Big Five here lion leopard Buffalo elephant and Rhino and the park is known for its extraordinarily Rich ecosystems and diversity of wildlife thanks to its location in a transition zone between the Lush lowveld vegetation and the parched Kalahari Desert apart from the big five the park is also home to African wild dogs Sable Antelope zebras and more than 300 species of birds strategically placed photographic hides make it easier to capture close-up photos to get the most out of a day trip to the park it's a good idea to sign up for the full day palanisburg National Park private tour from Johannesburg an experienced guide will take you into the park for both a morning and afternoon game Drive tracking the animals you most want to see and sharing fascinating details about the wildlife and unique ecosystems along the way at 7 the Cradle of humankind day trip about an hour's drive from Johannesburg the Cradle of humankind is a UNESCO world heritage site and one of the world's most productive and important paleoanthropological areas one of the top attractions here are the Stark Fontaine stalactitic caves which consist of six linked underground Chambers with a lake at a depth of about 40 meters archaeological excavations here on Earth the skull of a humanoid creature known as Australopithecus Africanus estimated to be 2 million years old called Mrs pleas and in 1998 scientists discovered a skeleton that dates the presence of early humans in the valley at three and a half million years ago stopped by The marapang Visitor Center to view related exhibits and learn more about this fascinating site better still explore all the highlights with an expert guide on the Cradle of humankind tour from Johannesburg this eight-hour Excursion includes a guided visit of the Stark Fontaine caves and The marapang Visitor Center as well as an open vehicle game drive at the lion and Safari Park foreign and Safari Park day trip at the lion and Safari Park you can get close up with some of Africa's famous Wildlife less than an hour's Drive North from downtown Johannesburg animal interactions are the Highlight here you can cuddle a lion cub take the cheetah walk and hand feed giraffes presumably you're on a ladder self-guided tours are permitted but you can also join a guided game Drive in the Park's vehicles to see Lions cheetahs zebras ostriches wild dogs hyenas and various species of antelope nighttime feeding tours and river rafting tours are other popular things to do make sure you adhere to all safety regulations and keep your windows up when driving through the park at 5 the maboneng precinct the vibrant mabaneng Precinct is a fantastic example of a successful mixed-use urban renewal project once a rather run-down neighborhood mabaring meaning a place of light now fizzes with life funky restaurants cafes art galleries shops hotels and entertainment venues mixed smartly with residential buildings a top attraction here is the weekly Market on Main with food from all over the continent arts on Main and Revolution house are two of the first developments where warehouses are transformed into artists Studios Galleries and shops other developments host bioscope and independent cinema a community center designer hotels and boutiques this is a wonderful area to wander around feel the vibe of the city and grab a bite to eat or a cool drink foreign house [Music] Soweto an abbreviation for southwestern townships something I didn't know lies 20 kilometers Southwest of Johannesburg and offers a journey into the soul for the fight for Freedom this was an area of planned segregation where black laborers were housed far from the city center many in Shacks made of corrugated iron it was also the birthplace of the struggle for democracy in 1976 the Soweto uprisings sought to overthrow the apartheid state and spread from here to the rest of the country today you can see a land of contrasts mansions have sprouted up among the shanties and the democratic government is trying to establish much needed infrastructure and green spaces popular attractions to visit here are Mandela house where Nelson Mandela lived from 1946 to 1962 and the hector Peterson Memorial and Museum which honors the brave students who protested apartheid during the Soweto uprisings some of whom were shot by police including the Museum's namesake who was only 12 years old the easiest and safest way to experience Soweto is on the full day Soweto City and apartheid Museum tour a knowledgeable guide will take you Into The Shanty towns to meet the residents and you'll have the chance to visit the famous villacazi street and the former homes of two Nobel Peace Prize winners Nelson Mandela and Archbishop Desmond Tutu as well as explore the excellent exhibits at the apartheid Museum and now at three Gold Reef City Gold Reef City eight kilometers from the city center takes you back to the Gold Rush days through a series of thrilling theme park rides and historical exhibits this family-friendly attraction hosts reproductions of buildings and businesses from the era and you can also tour a disused shaft of the crown mines one of the richest gold mines in the world children love the trampoline park Ten Pin bowling alley an exhilarating theme park rides including the twisting and turning Anaconda roller coaster the park also offers dedicated rides for young children as well as a petting zoo Putt-Putt golf Sideshow games and gold panning round out the adventures if you can't tear yourself away from all the fun you can stay overnight in the Gold Reef City Theme Park Hotel and now at two Constitution Hill overlooking the city of Johannesburg Constitution Hill is a former prison which provides fascinating insight into South Africa's history at the site you can explore provocative exhibits at the number four Museum the women's Jail Museum and the old fort museum together the precinct was once known as the fort and it forged a reputation for its brutal treatment of political prisoners common criminals and passive resistors famous former prisoners include Nelson Mandela and Mahatma Gandhi today the old awaiting trial building has been transformed into the Constitutional Court of South Africa a symbol of Freedom that works to protect the rights of all the nation's people the court welcomes visitors who want to attend hearings and watch the judicial process guided tours of constitutional Hill offer valuable insight into its Rich history foreign and finally at number one the apartheid Museum the apartheid Museum graphically portrays the apartheid story through photos artifacts newspaper clippings chilling personal accounts and film footage the sights and sounds of the apartheid era are sail visitors as they move through the thought-provoking permanent exhibits on an emotional journey through South Africa's history paths follow the country through Decades of Oppression to the birth of democracy Reserve at least two hours and preferably longer to get the most out of a visit here those interested in learning more about South Africa's struggle for Freedom should also visit Lilly sheep Farm Museum the former center for the leaders of the Liberation movement and there you have the top 10 rated attractions and things to do in Johannesburg did you like what you saw let us know in the comments down below share this video with your friends and don't forget to subscribe to our channel for more fantastic travel guides check out more videos on South Africa in our South African travel guide playlist that's all for now until next time

videos.title: The Truth About Living in Johannesburg, South Africa– A Locals Honest Opinion
videos.description:
Get ready to embark on the adventure of a lifetime as we unravel the top 10 reasons why you should plan your safari travel to South Africa. If you're a travel enthusiast with a thirst for exploring new horizons, South Africa is the ultimate destination that promises to satisfy your wanderlust.

Picture this: the magnificent landscapes, from the iconic Table Mountain to the awe-inspiring Drakensberg Mountains, set the stage for an unforgettable South African safari experience. With every twist and turn, you'll discover the allure of this captivating land.

As a traveler on a mission to embrace the wild, South Africa offers unrivaled wildlife safaris. These adventures take you deep into the heart of nature, where you'll encounter the incredible Big Five and a kaleidoscope of other mesmerizing creatures, bringing your safari dreams to life.

But South Africa is not just about the safari; it's a journey into the rich cultural heritage of this diverse nation. Explore the vibrant Zulu traditions of KwaZulu-Natal or uncover the historical tapestry of the Western Cape, each region narrating its unique story.

The historical landmarks of South Africa are like portals to the past. Places like Robben Island, where Nelson Mandela was imprisoned, and the Apartheid Museum provide profound insights into the nation's history, making your travel experience even more enlightening.

For thrill-seekers, the adventure activities in South Africa will pump your adrenaline. Experience the thrill of cage diving with great white sharks or take the plunge from the world's highest bungee jump, marking your journey with heart-pounding excitement.

Wine connoisseurs, on the other hand, will find their paradise in the Cape Winelands, where vineyards produce some of the world's most exquisite wines. Savor the flavors of South African terroir as you unwind amidst picturesque surroundings.

Travel to South Africa and explore the coastline, which boasts vibrant beaches in Durban and the untouched serenity of the Wild Coast, beckoning beach lovers to bask in the sun and surf.

Your culinary journey in South Africa is a gastronomic delight, with local delicacies and the cherished tradition of the braai, an authentic barbecue experience that brings people together around a sizzling fire.

Uncover the diverse urban vibes of South Africa's iconic cities, from the bustling streets of Johannesburg to the laid-back coastal charm of Durban and Cape Town, making your travel experiences truly unforgettable.

But the most precious gem of South Africa is its people. Their warmth and hospitality will envelop you as you traverse the country on your safari travel adventure.

So, if you're ready to make your dreams of safari travel to South Africa a reality, join us on this enchanting voyage. Start planning your epic journey today and experience the magic of this extraordinary land.

FOLLOW ME:

👉INSTAGRAM -  https://www.instagram.com/lifebybrad
👉TIK TOK -  https://www.tiktok.com/@lifebybrad
👉FACEBOOK - https://www.facebook.com/lifebybrad

If you’re reading this, I appreciate you more than you know!
Thank you for watching!
Don’t forget to like, subscribe, and turn on post notifications!

what south africa is really like
videos.transcript: I'm going to be giving you an insiders look into what Johannesburg is really like as someone who has loved J my whole life I'm excited to show you the authentic side of the city from its Dynamic culture to the day-to-day experiences let's dive in to the real Johannesburg before we dive into the details let me share a bit about my own experiences growing up ratia in Johannesburg yes I'm a Joe Burger through and through and I wouldn't have it any other way the city's vibrant energy diverse communities and Ever Changing Skyline has been my backdrop ever since day one I consider myself fortunate to have grown up and to still live in a relatively safe area Johannesburg like any other city has its challenges and safety concerns are a reality while there are areas that face difficulties I'm thankful to have been shielded from some of the harsher realities here growing up in Johannesburg there's always been a certain narrative about town the main city area yeah we were always told to be cautious especially considering the safety concerns it's a reality that many parts of the city face challenges and as someone who's grown up here I can attest to the importance of being aware of your surroundings personally I've ventured into town quite a few times and I can confirm that yes there are areas that require extra caution Johannesburg like many cities has its complexities and safety is a concern that cannot be overlooked however within the hearts of the city lies a tapestry of experiences waiting to be discovered places like maoning have transformed the urban landscape into a hub of creativity and cultural expression it's a must visit for anyone wanting to experience the vibrant art scene unique IES and the unmistakable energy that defines the city there are various tours in town that each teach you about our Rich history and diverse communities a place where I've been and recommend visiting is Victoria yards it's a place where artists designers artisians and small business manufactures Thrive as a community you can go there for coffee or just to enjoy the beautiful art that has been created there I highly recommend it was a fun time and also two tours that I haven't done but want to do are one the SEO tours and two to the City skate tours both look awesome especially the City skate tours I'm a skater myself and I would love going through the city on a skateboard one day one day it's actually crazy how I live here and there is still so much that I haven't done it just shows you how much there is actually to do here so my advice to anyone considering a visit be aware of your surroundings take the necessary precautions and most importantly explore it's about finding that balance between caution and curiosity Johannesburg may have challenges but it's a city that surprises and captivates those willing to look beyond the headlines I've been lucky to avoid many challenges faced by other parts of the city that's not to downplay the issues it's just a reminder that like any Metropolis Johannesburg is a tapestry of diverse experiences and I'm just here to share mine now when we talk about johannesburg's rich cultural tapestry it's like opening a treasure chest of diversity this city is a mosaic of cultures each contributing to the vibrant and dynamic culture that makes Johannesburg truly unique as someone has grown up here I've had the privilege of experiencing a multitude of cultural celebrations from the energetic beats of local festivals to the colorful and traditional rituals of various communities one of the most amazing things about johannisburg is the way their neighborhoods showcase their own cultural flavors whether you found yourself in the historic Streets of New Town the Hassan ble of forsburg or the artistic Haven of mville you encounter blend of traditions languages and and Cuisines that tell the story of South Africa's derse Heritage the markets like the neighborhoods Market in Brant or the rose ban arts and craft Market or like microcosms of diversity offering you a sensory Journey Through the sights sounds and tastes of South Africa it's not just a shopping experience it's a cultural emerence Johannesburg is also home to numerous cultural institutions such as theart Museum and Constitution Hill which provide invaluable insights into the country's history struggles and triumphs as a Dr Burger I've come to appreciate indulging in the vast array of delicious foods from the spacy flavors of bunny child to the comforting Taste of BOS at a local bra every dish carries a piece of South Africa's identity so when we talk about the rich cultural tapestry of Johannesburg it's not just a phrase It's a living breathing reality johannesburg's heartbeat is felt in the rhythm of everyday life mornings and chosy are a hustle and bustle a symphony of car engines and people on the Move where whether you're navigating the traffic on the iconic M1 highway or hopping on a h train to beat the city Rush The City comes alive with the promise of a new day as the day unfolds neighborhoods across Johannesburg showcase their distinct Personalities in Melville you might find the laid-back Vibe of students gathering in local coffee shops while in sansen the financial district the energy is one of high powered meetings and corporate Buzz as the sun sets jobber transforms into a city that never sleeps the night life is a mix of trendy rooftop bars live music venues and clubs whether you're dancing the night away in New Town or sipping cocktails with a view of the city Lots in Rose ban the energy is infectious and the KN is always full of possibilities but amidst the hustle and bassle of the city drberg also includes moments of Tranquility whether it's a quiet stroll through the botanical gardens or a lazy Sunday afternoon at one of the city's many cafes there's a balance between the fast-paced urban laugh and the Serene spaces that offer Retreats from the city energy it's a city that challenges and inspires a place where every street corner has a story and every day is a new chapter in The Adventure of urban living so there you have it that is what Johannesburg is really like from my perspective living in Johannesburg isn't just an experience it's a journey through cultures flavors and stories waiting to be discovered whether you're a Joe Burger remining about your own Adventures or just someone curious about this incredible City I hope this glimpse into everyday life has sparked your interest jerg is more than just a place on a map that's a feeling a spirit that captivates everyone who calls it hope hopefully you enjoyed this video if you did please give it a like subscribe if you're new and turn on post notifications so you don't miss the next video also leave a comment let me know what your thought of this video are you going to be traveling to South Africa is there something else you'd like me to make a video about about South Africa let me know I'm open to a conversation down below I will see you guys in the next video cheers

recommendations: Get ready to embark on the adventure of a lifetime as we unravel the top 10 reasons why you should plan your safari travel to South Africa

videos.title: 🖤 Worst & most dangerous part of Johannesburg. "Don't cross the bridge"... | South Africa travel
videos.description:
This video is a real and raw footage from the worst & most dangerous part of Johannesburg, South Africa. I’m taking you on a wild drive through the streets of the inner city that in recent times has become a no-go-zone for most residents and motorists. CBD is an abbreviation for central business district and it’s simply commercial and business center of the city. We are gonna be riding on the motorbike simply to be more mobile and as a result more secure. The ability of driving on a sidewalk if forced might also be an advantage during this trip. Robberies, muggings, hijackings even in the daylight have become a norm in Joburg CBD, with new videos popping up on the internet almost every week. Check out my experience from this dangerous spot.

-----------------------
Captions in english available, if they don’t appear automatically, switch them on. 
-----------
If you like this video, it would mean the world to me if you give it a thumbs up, comment on it and be sure to subscribe to my channel (red button below the video)! More inspiring people, meaningful life stories and off-the-beaten path adventures coming! Walk with me! 
----------------
Follow my travel adventures on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/pandora_path/
videos.transcript: [Music] But I'm going to listen to you, if you say its not good... I thought to go to the bridge. Maybe here around Braamfontein is safer, safer okay. -But what's going on there? They do what ever they want. There has been a substantial increase in murders and hijackings in Alexandra and Johannesburg cbd. The discovery of six dead bodies in a panel beating shop in the Joburg cbd. The hijacking of buildings in the city of Johannesburg remains a cause for concern, some of the residential properties have turned into hubs for criminal activity. Johannesburg Central police station recorded the most contact crimes of all police stations reported nationally. From robberies to theft and hijacked buildings these are just some of the daily crimes in the Johannesburg CBD. How do residents feel? You can't even complain to the police, you cant complain to anybody! There is no law in this country. I'm so ashamed to be a South African on this day in fact I denounced my South African connection! Hi guys welcome in South Africa, in this episode I'm gonna show you the most dangerous part of Johannesburg and according to me one of the worst in the whole world. I had three attempts to visit the city center. One short one at night then the second one on the bike during the day and the third one. When I try to find other people to walk the streets of CBD with me, this mission failed. I didn't find any volunteers, why? You will see for yourself in this episode. Ok having said that let's start from my first attempts in the car while we were stopped by the police blockade at the traffic lights. CBD is an abbreviation for central business district and it is simply commercial and business center of the city. Melville the drug dealers area. Stopped by a police. I just came for a drive to show the lady the town. I wanted to show her Joburg but I'm going to listen to you if you say its not good. Just friendly advice, that's why I'm asking where are you going? I thought to go there by the bridge. The bridge damn! Braamfontein I thought. Yes ok maybe here around Braamfontein is safe... It's safer. But deep by that bridge. The Nelson Mandela bridge. -What's going on there? The bridge no no no... But why? She doesn't believe me I told her it's not a good plan. Madam it's not safe. They break the windows, they can do whatever they want they can even take your car. They know maybe you have stuff like a phone or money. [Music] The bridge became an allegory of a boundary between Earth and the underworld. I don't have to mention that most of the white people I have met do not even attempt to go to the downtown, the ones that have to stay all the time in their cars with the windows fully closed or inside the fenced buildings. Majority of the ones that had wander out there were robbed often facing the gun pointed at them. And the light on. We are behind the bridge and hopefully everything is going well we are not gonna stay here long, for obvious reasons. We are with the bike it's going to be just quick stops not to trigger the danger. I guess yes we see how it goes. The reasoning behind going on the bike this time was simply to be more mobile and as a result more secure, the ability of driving on a sidewalk if forced might also be an advantage during this trip. Unfortunately as it quickly turned out there will be no stops, we didn't feel confident enough to jump off the motorbike anywhere. The passersby staring at us and the taxi drivers shaking their heads as if we were crazy compounded the feeling of uncertainty, but still it is gonna be an interesting drive through the streets of the inner city of Johannesburg. That in the recent times has become a no-go zone for most residents and motorists. Quick drive through Joburg CBD on a busy day reveals a vibrant economy dominated by markets, Street vendors, restaurants, pubs, liquor stores and the whole host of small businesses. Some of the historical Treasures business centers and eclectic architecture somewhat neglected but still impressive, testify to the former greatness and wealth when their continued mining of gold has firmly placed Johannesburg as an economical powerhouse of South Africa. [Music] Hello The majority of the residents in Johannesburg CBD are the South African Nationals who have been born in the Gauteng Province, they prefer to stay in Joburg inner city for the economic reasons as they can be closer to their workplaces. This number is followed by inhabitants who have migrated from another country, the smallest group are the people who have migrated from another province, most of the commerce had a dream of making it big in (the city of gold} and then their reality came. As a result the CBD looks over pressured due to large number of vehicles businesses and people. There is something wonderful about the city's lively streets there is also something troubling about what is happening behind the scenes. The city is a paradox of presence of entrepreneurship, art and culture hubs but simultaneously the lack of law enforcement and this has significantly contributed to the Urban Decay. The situation has been worsened over the past two decades as many companies and investors fled from the city center to Sandton mainly, the major intensifiers of this exodus where the endemic problems of crime and grime, unregulated street trading and the lack of proper accommodation for minibus taxis. The immigration from the CBD led to the phenomenon of buildings hijackings, when squatters move in abandoned buildings are colloquially said to have been hijacked. In some cases the hijackers are gangs who charge residents illegally, in other cases people just move in here without paying rent to anyone. Some of these hijacked buildings have been turned into hot spots for crime, prostitution and drug abuse. In November 2017 audit of about 500 bad buildings in the city of Johannesburg revealed that 134 were hijacked and that 24 of those actually belonged to the city itself. Okay I can finally take the helmet off we are in much safer area right now. [Music] What's the area called, yes what's the district called? Hi, we are wondering what's the area called the district what is it called? -Greenside. Ok thankyou. As the other day the policeman said ''white person on the street it means safe''. In this spot we are gonna end this exciting episode and walk with me to the next one.

videos.title: 10 things to know BEFORE visiting Johannesburg, South Africa (essential tips)

10 important things you need to know before taking a trip to Johannesburg, South Africa.

videos.transcript: hey friends welcome back to my channel if you're planning a trip to South Africa you've come to the right place I'm about to give you'all 10 essential tips and things you need to know in preparation for your trip so first thing to know in South Africa they use the South African RS okay so the currency is very different what y'all are going to do you're going to download this conversion app I use this app everywhere I go you do not need any internet connection or Wi-Fi once you download it it kind of just like has it all on your phone but you're able to convert any currency for any country so I use this everywhere I go so for example if I was to put in $100 it'll tell me that it's equivalent to about 1,900 South African Rands and that helps you if you want to like go to a market and they're telling you oh it's 50 RS you'll know how much that is in dollars it'll just help you pinpoint your prices but definitely recommend this app next important thing that I did not know so when you are paying with your card you're going to get two options it's going to be be straight and then you have budget and we didn't know what that meant like so literally whenever you like tap your card on the machine it'll say straight or budget and you'll select which one so straight means they're going to take it out immediately and budget is basically financing it so don't click budget I do want to mention a lot of places do take card but it's always good to have cash make sure you have some candy for remote locations or just small mom and pop shops that may not have credit card machines all right let's talk about Transportation if if you are American I am not going to tell you to go rent a car because they drive on the opposite side of the road don't even set yourself up for failure Ubers are very popular there if you're going to be doing Ubers especially at night Uber xl's are always going to be just a little safer and they're usually the nicer Vehicles so I would definitely recommend taking Uber XL if you're going somewhere very far or if you're by yourself traveling just to feel a little safer they also have local taxis that are run by the Zulu which are one of the tribes in South Africa to us it's it's not really a taxi it's more like a van and people just hop on and off but that's their equivalent of a taxi those are going to be really cheap but it's just going to be very tight no air and there's going to be lots of stops in between so it's like a little small mini bus and you're just stopping different places I would recommend take Ubers Ubers you can get from point A to point B number three if you're staying in an Airbnb I want y'all to be aware that there are so many safety protocols that you have to have a cold every time you go in and out of the Airbnb and it may not be the best if you have a big group of people because every person has to get a code and it's only valid for one use so once you leave you have to get another code and it's just a hassle my family and I did this when we went to South Africa um in December and it was just three of us but it was a lot because the cod's coming in and out if you want to leave more than once you need another Cod you have to keep asking the Airbnb host for these different cods that are generated every day so I would just say if you are in a big group definitely look at staying into Hotel because an Airbnb it's just going to be a lot of extra work let's talk about low shedding cuz this is a very very big Topic in South Africa if you don't know what load shedding is they are limited to their power and energy it cuts out in different places at different time and they call it load shedding so there is an instance where the electricity may cut off it could be hours honestly but 10 to 20 minutes and then it'll come back on and regenerate there's actually a website and an app that tells you the schedule of when the Lo shedding should be happening make sure you look at that and you can look at your area or your region and kind of see like how often does it low shed I would just recommend staying somewhere with um like a backup generator cuz the Airbnb that we stayed at we did not thankfully experience any load shedding it's so shocking we didn't experience any low shedding in our two weeks that we were there but we also made sure to stay somewhere that has that backup generator so that if the power does come out like it'll come back on shortly oh my gosh number four travel adapter and and this is where we went wrong we went to South Africa with our like International portable charger that we thought went to every country and you know we could switch it out there was no plug for South Africa and so the South African plug actually has the European plug and the European plug goes into their plug so you need two okay so I'm going to put the link in the description box of where you can order this on Amazon because y'all don't get down there and not have a a charger like we struggled with this the first day we got there all of our devices were literally dead because we could not plug anything in then we had to wait till the second day to try to find something in the mall the outlets are different and they're not going to be on most of the universal travel adapter so just make sure that you order it before you go but South Africa is very diverse it's actually known as the rainbow Nation so there's a bunch of different cultures different uh ethnicities I say that to say there are 12 official tribes the most common one is Zulu which which pretty much if you ask a local they tend to like run the city they are the ones that are like running these uh taxis be prepared to see so many different people so many different cultures lots of languages the most common language will be obviously English and Zulu so if you can speak English you should pretty much be able to get around I've picked up a few Zulu words and I'm actually like why do I want to learn Zulu like I want to be able to be fluent in Zulu it just sounds so cool so they say yo and SAA saona means hello next thing make sure you look at the weather because everywhere in South Africa has a different vibe we were in jerg it was very hot humid you definitely need sunscreen y'all like I got sunburn from just going outside for like an hour without protecting my face but if you go to Cape Town it's completely different it's very chilly very windy you need a coat so just make sure you do some research on what area you're going to be staying in if if you're visiting both joerg and Cape Town I would recommend bringing both summer clothes and then fall clothes I would say like a flannel a sweater something cuz it really does get chilly especially when you're near the water in Cape Town drinking is very common in South Africa and by drinking I mean like being drunk is normal there like being drunk is normal on a Tuesday night so I say that to say they have these random stops and searches on the road where the police will just randomly search you and it's for drunk driving this happened to us several times and our driver will be like oh they're just doing it to make sure you know there's no drunk drivers and especially at night so if you are going out or if you're like you know driving somewhere late at night I got a little nervous just because this happened to me in Ghana before and it was a whole setup but in joerg they are actually doing searches to make sure they're not not drunk drivers on the road so number 10 last thing I really want to talk about is the partying and clubbing scene like I said before South Africa they love to party okay they love to drink so expect to be out all night like they don't party and go home at 1:00 a.m. they are out to 4 5 6 7 .m. there was one night my sister and I literally stayed out until the place closed at 6:00 a.m. and the sun was up and we were like what is this so just be prepared to be outside be prepared to party it's going to be a good time they dance they like to have fun I don't know I like it better than American clubs cuz I feel like in America people just go to the club and they sit down and they just look cute and they're on their phone versus like my experience in joerg they're dancing they're vibing the music is good you know we love some I'm a piano over here so it's just it's just a really good time those are the 10 things that you should know before going to South Africa if this video helped you feel free to like leave a little comment let me know if you have any other questions I also did a week in Cape Town so check out some of my recent Vlogs and see how my trip went and I'll see you guys in the next video thanks for watching [Music] bye
